articles.summary: Unwrap [4] is the AI-powered customer intelligence platform trusted at scale by STRIPE, SOUTHWEST AIRLINES, PERPLEXITY, and other customer-centric companies.

CLAUDE FOR SMALL BUSINESS
articles.summary: Anthropic launched Claude for Small Business, a package of connectors and workflows that embeds Claude into tools like QuickBooks, PayPal, HubSpot, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365.

articles.title: NOTABLE RESEARCHERS JOIN $4 BILLION EFFORT TO BUILD SELF-IMPROVING AI
articles.summary: Recursive Superintelligence has raised more than $650 million at a valuation of more than $4 billion to build AI that can improve itself with little or no help from human developers. Its seven co-founders include notable researchers from many of the industry's leading AI companies. Many of these researchers specialize in AI systems that can run for long periods in pursuit of goals. While the researchers are bullish on the idea of AIs recursively improving themselves, the current technology is a long way from the point where humans can be removed from the loop.

articles.title: AI CHIPMAKER CEREBRAS RAISES $5.55 BILLION IN YEAR'S BIGGEST IPO
articles.summary: Cerebras raised $5.5 billion in its US IPO at a market valuation of about $40 billion. This was the largest IPO this year so far. The IPO drew orders for more than 20 times the number of shares available. The offering was led by Morgan Stanley, Citigroup, Barclays, and UBS.

articles.title: ANTHROPIC BEATS OPENAI ON BUSINESS ADOPTION
articles.summary: Anthropic has passed OpenAI in business adoption. More businesses used Anthropic than OpenAI in April. Anthropic has quadrupled business adoption over the last year, while OpenAI grew business adoption by only 0.3%. The pace of development in the AI industry is overriding the typical forces of vendor stickiness.

articles.title: THE ECONOMICS OF SUPERSTAR AI RESEARCHERS
articles.summary: Superstar researchers at frontier labs can earn over a hundred times more than the average AI postdoc. Researcher quantity doesn't easily make up for quality in the field of AI. Even a 2x researcher can earn far more than the median because their contributions easily scale to billions of users. If they can add something that multiple 1x researchers can't, then it's worth paying a lot to capture it.

articles.title: HOW OPENAI BUILT THE CODEX WINDOWS SANDBOX
articles.summary: OpenAI detailed the engineering behind Codex's Windows sandbox, which constrained local commands, file access, and networking permissions while still allowing coding agents to operate effectively on developer machines.

articles.title: AI GATEWAY PRODUCTION TRENDS
articles.summary: Vercel analyzed seven months of AI Gateway production traffic spanning hundreds of models and over 200,000 teams. The report showed rapid growth in agentic workloads, increasing adoption of open-source models, and heavy multi-model routing in large-scale deployments.

articles.title: AGENT FOUNDRY: RUN CLAUDE CODE, OPENCLAW, AND OTHER AGENTS ON A CENTRALIZED + SECURE INSTANCE
articles.summary: Agents are a mess to manage. With Agent Foundry [13], you get a single place to govern agents like OpenClaw and Claude Code. Real enforcement at the agent level, deployed in your own environment or SentinelOne's for zero lock-in. Powered by Prompt Security from

articles.title: SentinelOne. Join the waitlist
CLINE RELEASES OPEN-SOURCE AGENT RUNTIME SDK FOR CODING AGENTS
articles.summary: @cline/sdk is an open-source framework for building agentic applications. It has a plugin architecture that makes it easy to customize, and the framework has all the features expected from agents, like checkpoints, web fetch, MCPs, cron jobs, subagents, and more. The SDK can be used to run agents from CI/CD pipelines, create automations for end-to-end workflows, or embed agents directly inside products.

articles.title: PYTORCH 2.12 RELEASE HIGHLIGHTS
articles.summary: PyTorch 2.12 shipped major infrastructure updates including faster CUDA eigendecomposition, a unified graph capture API, MX quantization export support, and fused Adagrad optimizers.

articles.title: SECURITY ARCHITECTURE BEHIND PERPLEXITY COMPUTER
KRISHNA RAO PODCAST APPEARANCE
articles.summary: Krishna Rao, Anthropic's CFO, joined the company two years ago when run-rate revenue was about $250 million. It is now $30 billion. Rao helped raise around $75 billion and is responsible for the procurement and allocation of compute. This post links to an interview with Rao where he discusses compute, raising funds, pricing dynamics, how Anthropic's finance team uses Claude, Mythos, biotech and healthcare, and much more.

articles.title: MICROSOFT'S MULTI-AGENT AI SYSTEM TOPS ANTHROPIC'S MYTHOS ON CYBERSECURITY BENCHMARK
articles.summary: Microsoft's MDASH AI system uses more than 100 specialized AI agents to work together across multiple AI models to find real-world software vulnerabilities. A set of agents scans code for potential vulnerabilities, and then a separate group of agents debates whether each finding is real and exploitable. A final stage constructs proof-of-concept attacks to confirm the bugs exist. MDASH surpassed Anthropic's Mythos model on the CyberGym benchmark, a test that measures how well AI systems can reproduce real-world vulnerabilities.

GOOGLE PLANS TO ANNOUNCE A NEW GEMINI MODEL
articles.summary: The model, which will be announced at Google's annual I/O conference on Tuesday, will roughly be on par with GPT-5.5.

articles.title: ADAPTION AIMS BIG WITH AUTOSCIENTIST, AN AI TOOL THAT HELPS MODELS TRAIN THEMSELVES
articles.summary: AutoScientist helps models learn specific capabilities quickly by using an automated approach to conventional fine-tuning.

articles.title: WE TESTED DEEPSEEK V4 PRO AND FLASH AGAINST CLAUDE OPUS 4.7 AND KIMI K2.6
articles.summary: DeepSeek V4 Pro scored 77/100 on the FlowGraph spec for $2.25 and lands between Opus 4.7 and Kimi K2.6 in terms of performance.

articles.title: PAID CLAUDE PLANS CAN CLAIM A DEDICATED MONTHLY CREDIT
articles.summary: Paid Claude plans will be able to claim a dedicated monthly credit for programmatic usage starting on June 15.
